steps.assistant_text: rmon read-memory command handler (kernel_018_b, IDO 7.1 -O1). Matched byte-exact from a near-miss structural decode (~84.5%) via four levers: (1) 'register s32 var_s0/var_s1;' forces the two SP-range validity flags into callee-saved s0/s1 (frame 0x40, both saved) and lets the token-poll loop accumulator reuse s0 after the flags die (matching 'move s0,v0'); (2) routing the loop count via 'var_s0 = func_800066F0(...); sp38 += var_s0;' reuses s0 rather than promoting a 3rd s-reg; (3) declaration order (p, sp38, sp34, sp30, sp2C, addr) pins IDO's reverse stack-slot numbering to the target layout (addr=0x28, ub=0x2C, arg0=0x30, sp34=0x34, sp38=0x38, p=0x3C); (4) writing the SP-window bound as 'FW(0x14)+FW(0x10)' (len+addr) fixes the commutative addu operand order, and ordering 'sp34 = len+0x10;' BEFORE 'p = &sp34;' fixes the token-loop load/store scheduling so the len reload fills the load-delay slot instead of the p store. Inlining the field reads (no temp_t0/temp_t4 locals) removed two spurious cross-branch spills. .text byte-identical; ROM cmp clean.

metadata.verification: Full make clean && make produces ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp clean). func_80006790 .text byte-identical to expected/src/kernel/kernel_018_b.c.o (132 words, 0 diffs).
